title: fastGRO Protocol for Nuclear Run-On Assay
author: Elisa Barbieri
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bbmgik3w

steps.title: Harvest cells and wash in cold 1X PBS
steps.instruction: Harvest cells and wash in cold 1X PBS

steps.title: Prepare fresh 2x Nuclear run-on buffer (NRO)
steps.instruction: Prepare fresh 2x Nuclear run-on buffer (NRO). ( 100 µL /sample) 10 mM Tris-HCl ph8 5 mM MgCl 2 300 mM KCl 1 mM DTT 500 μM ATP 500 μM GTP 500 μM 4-thio-UTP 2 μM CTP 200 μ/ml Superase-in 1% Sarkosyl (N-Laurylsarcosine sodium salt solution) Per library, use 1.5-2 x10^7 nuclei

steps.title: Centrifuge 900 x g, °C, 6...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 900 x g, °C, 6 minutes and resuspend using wide-end pipette tips in FB to a concentration of 2x10^7 nuclei per 10 μl of FB.

steps.title: Nuclei can be stored at -80 °C for months
steps.instruction: Nuclei can be stored at -80 °C for months.

steps.title: Remove supernatant and resuspend in 10 mL of FB
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant and resuspend in 10 mL of FB. Take 10 µL for cell count.

steps.title: Flick to loosen pellet and resuspend...
steps.instruction: Flick to loosen pellet and resuspend in 25 mL of LyB. Centrifuge 600 x g, °C, 5 minutes

steps.title: Incubate on ice for 00:05:00 Add...
steps.instruction: Incubate on ice for 00:05:00 Add 25 mL of LyB and centrifuge 600 x g, °C, 5 minutes .

steps.title: Vortex lightly while adding 10 mL of LyB
steps.instruction: Vortex lightly while adding 10 mL of LyB

steps.title: Remove supernatant and resuspend in 10...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant and resuspend in 10 mL GSB Volume of GSB should be at least 5 times the volume of cell pellet

steps.title: Warm the NRO buffer at 30 °C
steps.instruction: Warm the NRO buffer at 30 °C .

steps.title: Add 160 µL of chloroform, shake...
steps.instruction: Add 160 µL of chloroform, shake vigorously by hand for 00:00:15

steps.title: Incubate 00:02:00 at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate 00:02:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12000 rpm, 4 °C, 15 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12000 rpm, 4 °C, 15 minutes .

steps.title: Transfer upper, aqueous phase into new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer upper, aqueous phase into new 1.5 mL centrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add 400 µL of isopropanol to...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL of isopropanol to precipitate RNA and incubate at Room temperature for 00:10:00 . Can add 1-2 ul of 2 μg/μL glycogen to allow for visualization of pellet with lower RNA concentrations.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12000 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12000 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes .

steps.title: Wash RNA pellet using 1 mL...
steps.instruction: Wash RNA pellet using 1 mL of cold 75 % ethanol Centrifuge at 12000 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes .

steps.title: Completely remove ethanol and air-dry pellet
steps.instruction: Completely remove ethanol and air-dry pellet. Dissolve in 100 µL of nuclease-free water.

steps.title: Determine concentration by Nanodrop or Qubit
steps.instruction: Determine concentration by Nanodrop or Qubit.

steps.title: Resuspend cells in 10 mL of ice-cold SB
steps.instruction: Resuspend cells in 10 mL of ice-cold SB. Incubate for 00:05:00 . Spin 400 x g, °C, 10 minutes .

steps.title: Mix 100 µL of thawed nuclei...
steps.instruction: Mix 100 µL of thawed nuclei solution with 100 µL of 2xNRO buffer. Pipette up and down 15 times using end-cut pipette tip.

steps.title: Incubate 00:07:00 at 30 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate 00:07:00 at 30 °C .

steps.title: Add 600 µL Trizol LS
steps.instruction: Add 600 µL Trizol LS. Vortex. Incubate 00:05:00 at Room temperature . STOP POINT: Freeze with liquid nitrogen, and store at -80 °C

steps.title: Thaw nuclei On ice
steps.instruction: Thaw nuclei On ice . 5-10% drosophila nuclei can be added to your sample as spike-in if not using 4S-U labelled drosophila RNA in step 26.

steps.title: Transfer 150 µg of RNA to a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer 150 µg of RNA to a 1.5 ml tube and add water up to 500 µL . Save 5 μl of unfragmented RNA to be run on TapeStation as a control for fragmented RNA and to check quality of RNA.

steps.title: Transfer 150 µg of fragmented RNA...
steps.instruction: Transfer 150 µg of fragmented RNA in one 2 ml Eppendorf tube. Use only polypropylene tubes during biotinylation.

steps.title: Add appr
steps.instruction: Add appr. 800 µL of chloroform to the RNA-biotin in the 2 mL phase-lock tube and mix by manually shaking the tube.

steps.title: Prepare Wash Buffer (WB)
steps.instruction: Prepare Wash Buffer (WB): 100 mM Tris pH 7.5 10 mM EDTA pH 8.0 1M NaCl 0.1% (vol/vol) Tween-20

steps.title: Use buffers provided with the RNA...
steps.instruction: Use buffers provided with the RNA Clean and Purification kit-5 (Zymo Research). Add ethanol to wash and pre-wash buffers and resuspend DNAse in water.

steps.title: Add 5-10% of labelled spike-in RNA...
steps.instruction: Add 5-10% of labelled spike-in RNA if using instead of drosophila nuclei.

steps.title: Fragment RNA using Bioruptor with the...
steps.instruction: Fragment RNA using Bioruptor with the following settings using: 1 cycle: 30 sec / 30 sec ON / OFF at high settings.

steps.title: Transfer fragmented RNA to 2 ml tube
steps.instruction: Transfer fragmented RNA to 2 ml tube.

steps.title: Analyze fragmentation efficiency of fragmented versus...
steps.instruction: Analyze fragmentation efficiency of fragmented versus unfragmented RNA on Agilent 2200 TapeStation.

steps.title: Sonicated RNA can be snap-frozen in...
steps.instruction: Sonicated RNA can be snap-frozen in liquid nitrogen and stored at -80 °C

steps.title: Incubate in the dark at 24...
steps.instruction: Incubate in the dark at 24 °C and 800 rpm, °C, for 02:00:00 .

steps.title: Libraries can be prepared with NEBNext...
steps.instruction: Libraries can be prepared with NEBNext Ultra II Directional RNA Library Prep or other library prep kits.

steps.title: Measure concentration of labelled RNA by Qubit fluorometer
steps.instruction: Measure concentration of labelled RNA by Qubit fluorometer.

steps.title: Repeat steps 57-58 and collect the...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 57-58 and collect the eluted RNA in the same tube ( 200 µL total volume).

steps.title: Prepare Biotin-RNA mix in 2 ml tube. Follow the order
steps.instruction: Prepare Biotin-RNA mix in 2 ml tube. Follow the order: up to 150 μg of fragmented RNA in 500 µL 100 µL Biotinylation Buffer 200 µL DMF 200 µL EZ-link HPDP Biotin

steps.title: Incubate RNA at 65 °C for...
steps.instruction: Incubate RNA at 65 °C for 00:10:00 , then On ice for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Transfer tubes to the magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Transfer tubes to the magnetic rack. Collect the 4-thio-labeled RNA in a new tube.

steps.title: Resuspend beads in in 100 µL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend beads in in 100 µL of 100 mM DTT and incubate 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Wash at least 3 times with...
steps.instruction: Wash at least 3 times with 900 µL of room temperature WB.

steps.title: Wash at least 3 times with...
steps.instruction: Wash at least 3 times with 900 µL of warm ( 65 °C ) WB.

steps.title: Transfer tubes to a magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Transfer tubes to a magnetic rack. Remove supernatant. Do not disturb beads.

steps.title: Incubate at 4 °C for 00:15:00 in rotation
steps.instruction: Incubate at 4 °C for 00:15:00 in rotation.

steps.title: Add 100 µL of prepared Invitrogen...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of prepared Invitrogen streptavidin beads to 200 µL of RNA-biotin.

steps.title: Increase the volume of the solution...
steps.instruction: Increase the volume of the solution of RNA-biotin to 200 µL with nuclease-free water. Can also scale down to 100 μL if you have less than 150 μg and use 50uL of beads.

steps.title: Incubate at 65 °C for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at 65 °C for 00:10:00 . Place on ice for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Prepare the M280 Streptavidin Dynabeads:
steps.instruction: Prepare the M280 Streptavidin Dynabeads:

steps.title: Take 100 µL of beads per sample
steps.instruction: Take 100 µL of beads per sample.

steps.title: Wash the beads twice with 2...
steps.instruction: Wash the beads twice with 2 volumes ( 200 µL per sample) of wash buffer.

steps.title: Resuspend in 1 Volume ( 100...
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 1 Volume ( 100 µL per sample) of wash buffer.

steps.title: Leave half volume of WB at...
steps.instruction: Leave half volume of WB at Room temperature and heat the other half at 65 °C .

steps.title: Resuspend RNA in 100 µL of nuclease-free water
steps.instruction: Resuspend RNA in 100 µL of nuclease-free water. Biotinylated RNA can be stored at -80 °C .

steps.title: Spin quickly at 4 °C and...
steps.instruction: Spin quickly at 4 °C and remove remaining supernatant with 200 μl and 10 μl pipettes. Biotinylated RNA should NOT dry.

steps.title: Remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant.

steps.title: Wash pellet with 1 mL of ice-cold 75% ethanol
steps.instruction: Wash pellet with 1 mL of ice-cold 75% ethanol. Centrifuge 10006 x g, 4 °C, 30 minutes .

steps.title: Remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant.

steps.title: Centrifuge 16000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 16000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes .

steps.title: Add 6 µL DNase/RNase-Free Water directly...
steps.instruction: Add 6 µL DNase/RNase-Free Water directly to the column matrix, incubate for 00:01:00 and centrifuge for 00:00:30

steps.title: Centrifuge for 00:01:00 at full speed...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 00:01:00 at full speed to ensure complete removal of the wash buffer. Transfer the column carefully into an RNase-free tube.

steps.title: Add 1 volume (1 ml) of...
steps.instruction: Add 1 volume (1 ml) of isopropanol and mix for 00:00:15 manually.

steps.title: Add 1/10 volume (100 μl) of...
steps.instruction: Add 1/10 volume (100 μl) of 5 M NaCl and mix. If needed at 1-2 μL of 2 μg/μL Glycogen to allow for visualization of pellet with lower RNA concentrations.

steps.title: Transfer upper phase into new tube (appr 1 mL)
steps.instruction: Transfer upper phase into new tube (appr 1 mL).

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4 °C and 16000 x g, °C, 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4 °C and 16000 x g, °C, 00:05:00

steps.title: Add 400 µL RNA Wash Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL RNA Wash Buffer to the column and centrifuge for 00:02:00 . Discard the flow-through.

steps.title: Add 700 µL RNA Wash Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 700 µL RNA Wash Buffer to the column and centrifuge for 00:00:30 . Discard the flow-through.

steps.title: Add 400 µL RNA Prep Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL RNA Prep Buffer to the column and centrifuge for 00:00:30 . Discard the flow-through.

steps.title: Add 40 µL reaction mix directly to the column matrix
steps.instruction: Add 40 µL reaction mix directly to the column matrix. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:15:00 .

steps.title: Add 400 µL of RNA Binding...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL of RNA Binding Buffer to each sample and mix.

steps.title: Add 600 µL of ethanol (95-100%) and mix
steps.instruction: Add 600 µL of ethanol (95-100%) and mix.

steps.title: Transfer the sample to the Zymo-Spin...
steps.instruction: Transfer the sample to the Zymo-Spin IC Column in a Collection Tube and centrifuge for 00:00:30 . Discard the flow-through.

steps.title: Add 400 µL of RNA Wash...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL of RNA Wash Buffer to the column and centrifuge for 16000 x g, °C, 30 seconds . Discard the flow-through.

steps.title: For each sample to be treated,...
steps.instruction: For each sample to be treated, prepare DNase I reaction mix in an RNase-free tube. Mix well by gentle inversion: 5 µL DNase I 35 µL DNA Digestion Buffer
